title: Golden Polish Pierogi Dumplings
description: Tangy and flaky Polish dish featuring malt vinegar and bhuna, perfect for midnight cravings. This ovo-vegetarian friendly recipe serves as a delicious snack.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, warm water, and egg to form a smooth dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes elastic.
Divide the dough into smaller pieces and roll each into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of grated potato and chopped onion in the center of each circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and gently add the pierogi.
Cook the pierogi for 5 minutes or until they float to the surface.
While the pierogi are cooking, melt the butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the malt vinegar and bhuna spice mix to the melted butter and stir to combine.
Serve the pierogi in a Korean brass bowl with the tangy butter sauce spooned over the top.
